Title: Exploiting Natural Variation in Tomato to Define Pathway Structure and Metabolic Regulation of Fruit Polyphenolics in the Lycopersicum Complex

Pathway_Genes.Variety: M82; MicroTom; Arabidopsis Col-0 used for heterologous overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Flavonoid/flavonol glycosylation branch of polyphenol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Quercetin; kaempferol and other flavonoid aglycones; UDP-glucose
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to the 3-O position of flavonoids/flavonols to form flavonoid 3-O-glucosides
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate glycosylated products that serve as precursors for further rhamnosylation/apiosylation/7-O-glucosylation and promote fruit polyphenolic accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Tomato fruit polyphenolics, especially flavonol glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of flavonol-3-O-glucosides and downstream glycosylated flavonols
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Predicted/observed active in tomato fruits and leaves; conserved across LYCO, PIMP, NEOR, HABR, and PENN; silencing tested in MicroTom fruit
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stage; natural variation across tomato species/tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay; Arabidopsis overexpression and complementation-like test in f3gt background; tomato transient overexpression; tomato VIGS silencing; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key decoration step; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc10g083440 was identified as the major candidate tomato flavonoid 3-O-glucosyltransferase from comparative metabolomics, phylogeny, and expression analysis. 2. Recombinant protein glycosylated quercetin using UDP-glucose. 3. Overexpression in Arabidopsis increased flavonol-3Glc-related compounds, while VIGS suppression in tomato decreased flavonol-3-O-glucosides and transient overexpression increased them.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to elevate tomato flavonol glycosides by overexpression or allele deployment; can be stacked with downstream decoration genes to diversify fruit polyphenols
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper also notes this gene had previously been annotated as an anthocyanin 3-O-glucosyltransferase in purple tomato analysis; active PSPG sites were highly conserved across several tomato species.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS metabolomics; public RNA-seq from Koenig et al. 2013 and Bolger et al. 2014; TOM2 microarray

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Flavonoid/flavonol glycosylation branch of polyphenol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Flavonol 7-O-glucosylated derivatives such as Q3Glc6''Rha7Glc-related compounds
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Proposed to transfer glucose to the 7-O position of flavonoids/flavonols
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: adds terminal decoration to downstream flavonol glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruits and leaves, absent in some species/tissues such as PENN
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to accumulation of flavonol 7-O-glucosides, but functional evidence remains incomplete
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Predicted from metabolite absence/presence; PENN fruits and leaves lack flavonol-7-O-glucosides suggesting absent/non-expressed gene; candidate expression supported in HABR seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative metabolomics; phylogenetic analysis of tomato UGTs; VIGS/transient overexpression in tomato
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key downstream decoration step; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc10g079350 was proposed as the best candidate for tomato flavonoid 7-O-glucosyltransferase from pathway reconstruction and phylogeny. 2. Metabolite absence in PENN supported loss/non-expression of this activity in that species. 3. Transient overexpression caused only a relatively low effect and the authors state that further characterization is required.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to diversify flavonol glycoside structures, but should be validated before deployment
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The article contains inconsistent UGT subfamily naming around this candidate; regardless, Solyc10g079350 is the proposed F7GlcT. Sequence similarity to known F7GTs is low, so assignment is tentative.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS metabolomics; public RNA-seq; UGT phylogeny

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Flavonoid/flavonol glycosylation branch of polyphenol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Proposed to add apiose to the glucose moiety of flavonol 3-O-glucosides
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms decorated intermediates leading to higher-order flavonol glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruits and leaves in species showing these metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes accumulation of apiosylated flavonol glycosides; absence/lack of expression inferred in CHMI and NEOR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Highly expressed in HABR leaves; absent or not expressed in CHMI and NEOR based on metabolite patterns
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative metabolomics; RNA-seq expression comparison; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key decoration step; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc10g008860 clustered near flavonoid-3Glc-2''-O-glycosyltransferases and was annotated as the candidate F3G2''ApiT. 2. Its expression pattern matched species/tissue accumulation of flavonol-3Glc-2''Api derivatives. 3. Lack of these metabolites in CHMI and NEOR suggested absence or non-expression of this activity in those species.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate gene for engineering apiosylated flavonol diversity in tomato fruit
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function is inferred from phylogeny plus metabolite/species patterns; no direct biochemical validation in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS metabolomics; public RNA-seq; phylogenetic analysis

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Flavonoid/flavonol glycosylation branch of polyphenol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Rutinoside-like flavonol derivatives such as quercetin/kaempferol-3Glc6''Rha
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers rhamnose to the 6'' position of the glucose moiety on flavonoid 3-O-glucosides
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms major downstream flavonol rutinosides and affects further decoration products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Tomato flavonol glycosides including rutin/nicotiflorin-type derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of 3Glc6''Rha-containing flavonols; lower expression associated with HABR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Clearly lower in HABR and higher in PENN according to in silico expression analysis; lower expression in HABR correlated with low kaempferol/quercetin-3Glc6''Rha and Is3Glc6''Rha accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative metabolomics; in silico RNA-seq expression comparison
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch decoration step; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc09g059170 was annotated as tomato Fd3G6''RhaT. 2. Reduced expression in HABR explained low accumulation of 3Glc6''Rha derivatives. 3. Higher expression in PENN matched stronger predicted rhamnosylation capacity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to alter rutin/nicotiflorin-type flavonol glycoside composition in fruit
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct enzymatic validation in this paper; assignment is supported by pathway logic and transcript-metabolite correlation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Proposed to add two glucose residues by C-glycosylation to phloretin at the 3' and 5' positions
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms the detected dihydrochalcone glycoside P35diGlc
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Mature fruits of red-fruited species, PERU, and HABR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely enables accumulation of P35diGlc in species carrying this branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Orthology-based annotation from known citrus/rice/apple related genes; metabolite-pathway matching
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc02g088500 was identified as the tomato ortholog of characterized flavonoid C-glycosyltransferases. 2. The gene was assigned to the phloretin-3',5'-C-diglucosylation reaction producing P35diGlc. 3. This helps explain species-specific dihydrochalcone accumulation in tomato fruits.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate to engineer rare dihydrochalcone derivatives in tomato
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assignment is based on orthology and pathway reconstruction; direct functional validation was not provided in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Flavonoid methylation branch of polyphenol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Methylated quercetin derivatives including isorhamnetin glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Proposed to methylate hydroxyl groups of quercetin-type flavonols to generate isorhamnetin derivatives
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: produces methylated flavonol branch contributing to isorhamnetin glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes isorhamnetin derivative accumulation, which is exceptionally high in HABR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metabolite annotation and orthologous gene assignment
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch step for methylated flavonols
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc03g080180 was annotated as OMT1 in the refined tomato polyphenol pathway. 2. This step explains accumulation of methylated quercetin/isorhamnetin glycosides. 3. The branch is particularly prominent in HABR, which accumulates high isorhamnetin.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance methylated flavonol composition in tomato fruit
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct biochemical validation was not shown in this paper; methylated products were identified by MS and co-elution logic.
